split_environment.instruction: Split one source formal environment into atomic formal environments under DESIGN.md. Do not generate remark blocks in this step.

atomic_items.title: Atomic display title
atomic_items.label: correct-prefix:stable-slug
atomic_items.source_fragment: small source fragment this item came from

hard_rules: Each atomic item must contain exactly one independently extractable mathematical item.
Do not use canonical predicate names or \operatorname{...} in formal_body.
Do not bundle paired notions, operation lists, or multiple cases into one item.
Every item must have exactly one label with the correct environment prefix.
Do not generate Standard/Predicate/Negation/Failure/Interpretation blocks here.

generate_definition.instruction: Generate one atomic formal environment in house notation from one atomic item.
hard_rules: Use standard mathematical notation only in the formal body.
Do not use canonical predicate names or \operatorname{...}.
Include exactly one label immediately after the environment heading.
Do not include any remark blocks.
Do not include proof text.

generate_standard_quantified_statement.instruction: Generate the Standard quantified statement remark for one atomic formal environment.
hard_rules: Use standard mathematical notation only.
Do not use canonical predicate names or \operatorname{...}.
Declare all newly quantified variable domains explicitly.
If the display is multi-clause or long, use an aligned environment.
Do not include explanatory prose; prose belongs in Interpretation or Failure modes.

generate_definition_predicate_reading.instruction: Generate the Definition predicate reading remark if a canonical predicate exists or clearly applies.

no_predicate_json_schema.reason: No canonical predicate exists or the quantified form is clearer without one.

hard_rules: Use only predicate names present in predicates.yaml.
Use operator-style predicate names: \operatorname{Name}(...).
Do not invent predicates locally.
The predicate reading must be logically closed unless parameters are fixed by the immediately preceding statement.

generate_negated_quantified_statement.instruction: Generate the Negated quantified statement remark for one atomic formal environment when the negation is mathematically illuminating.

omit_json_schema.reason: The negation is not mathematically illuminating for this item.

hard_rules: Use standard mathematical notation only.
Do not use canonical predicate names or \operatorname{...}.
The block contains the formal negation only.
Do not explain the negation in prose here.
Push negations through quantifiers when this clarifies the failure condition.

generate_negation_predicate_reading.instruction: Generate the Negation predicate reading remark when a Negated quantified statement was generated and canonical predicates apply.

no_predicate_json_schema.reason: No canonical predicate reading applies to the negation.

hard_rules: Use only predicate names present in predicates.yaml.
Use operator-style predicate names: \operatorname{Name}(...).
Do not invent predicates locally.
This block may use predicate language because its role permits it.

generate_failure_modes.instruction: Generate a Failure modes remark when the negation has distinct mathematically meaningful branches.

omit_json_schema.reason: The negation has no useful branch decomposition.

hard_rules: Use prose or a short itemize list.
If the body begins with itemize, include \hfill immediately after the remark heading.
Do not introduce new definitions.
Do not use predicate language unless the remark is explicitly analyzing predicate failure.

generate_failure_mode_decomposition.instruction: Generate a Failure mode decomposition remark when failure modes benefit from formal decomposition.

omit_json_schema.reason: The failure modes do not require a formal decomposition display.

hard_rules: Use aligned displays for long or multi-branch decompositions.
Canonical predicates are allowed only when the block is explicitly predicate-analysis oriented.
Do not duplicate prose from Failure modes.

generate_contrapositive_quantified_state.instruction: Generate a Contrapositive quantified statement only when it is mathematically illuminating.

omit_json_schema.reason: The contrapositive is not useful enough to include.

hard_rules: Use standard mathematical notation only.
Do not use canonical predicate names or \operatorname{...}.
Use aligned displays if the statement is long.
Do not include generic contrapositives that add no mathematical insight.

generate_interpretation.instruction: Generate the Interpretation remark for one atomic formal environment.
hard_rules: Use prose.
Do not introduce new formal content.
Do not use predicate-language notation unless explicitly discussing notation.
Keep the interpretation concise and explanatory.

generate_dependencies.instruction: Generate the Dependencies remark for one atomic formal environment using the provided formal-item label catalog.
hard_rules: Use explicit \hyperref[...] labels from the provided formal-item label catalog.
Do not reference proof labels such as prf:...
Use No local dependencies. only for items that are foundational within the current extraction scope.
Do not infer a dependency merely from thematic similarity.
Do include definitions directly used by the statement, notation, proof idea, or standard quantified form.
If the body begins with itemize, include \hfill immediately after the remark heading.

rewrite_formal_body.instruction: Rewrite a noncompliant formal environment body into standard mathematical notation.
hard_rules: Remove predicate-language notation from forbidden roles.
Preserve mathematical meaning.
Do not make a bundled environment atomic by hiding multiple concepts in prose.
If the body is bundled, return needs_split=true rather than rewriting it as one block.

rewrite_logical_block.instruction: Rewrite a logical block for its stated role under DESIGN.md.
hard_rules: Standard, negated, and contrapositive quantified statements must use standard mathematical notation only.
Predicate names belong only in predicate-reading or explicit predicate-analysis blocks.
Interpretation blocks must be prose only.

assemble_atomic_block_stack.instruction: Assemble one complete atomic formal block stack after all component generations pass validation.
hard_rules: Preserve DESIGN.md logical block order.
Omit optional blocks only when their generation step returned an explicit omit/no_predicate result.
Do not insert prose between the formal environment and its attached logical blocks.
The Dependencies block is mandatory and comes after Interpretation.
